title: US-020 — CI/CD Pipeline & Release: scan → publish to ghcr.io, semantic versioning
description: As a DevOps engineer, I want an automated pipeline that builds, tests, scans, and publishes the image so that every merge to main is immediately deployable.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Pipeline stages: lint → type-check → unit test → integration test → build → scan → publish
- [ ] Image tagged with git SHA and semantic version tag
- [ ] Published to ghcr.io on merge to main
- [ ] Release notes auto-generated from conventional commits
- [ ] Secrets managed via GitHub Secrets (never in pipeline YAML)

title: US-017 — Observability: structured JSON logs, Prometheus metrics, health endpoints
description: As a DevOps engineer, I want structured JSON logs and Prometheus metrics so that I can monitor and alert on service health in production.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] All logs in JSON format with: timestamp, level, traceId, message, context
- [ ] API keys NEVER appear in logs (masked to ***)
- [ ] Prometheus metrics at /metrics: request count, latency histograms, provider call counters, error rate
- [ ] /health/live always returns 200
- [ ] /health/ready returns 200 if provider reachable, 503 otherwise
- [ ] Unit test: log output for a tool call contains no secret values
- [ ] Integration test: /metrics returns valid Prometheus text format

title: US-016 — Rate Limiting: per-client token bucket, configurable limit
description: As a security champion, I want per-client rate limiting on the MCP endpoint so that the service is protected from abuse and excessive API costs.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Rate limit configurable via MAX_REQUESTS_PER_MINUTE (default: 60)
- [ ] Exceeding limit returns MCP error: "rate limit exceeded, retry after Xs"
- [ ] Rate limit tracked per client IP (or MCP session ID)
- [ ] Unit test: 61st request within 60s window → rate limit error
- [ ] Unit test: rate limit resets after the window

title: US-019 — Docker Image: multi-stage Dockerfile, non-root, Trivy scan
description: As a DevOps engineer, I want a minimal, hardened OCI image so that the service can be deployed anywhere containers run.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Multi-stage Dockerfile: build (full Bun) → runtime (slim)
- [ ] Runtime image ≤ 200 MB
- [ ] Runs as non-root user (uid 1000)
- [ ] HEALTHCHECK instruction in Dockerfile
- [ ] Image passes Trivy scan with zero HIGH/CRITICAL vulnerabilities
- [ ] Supports linux/amd64 and linux/arm64 (multi-platform build)
- [ ] Container test: start → health check passes → tools/list call succeeds

title: US-018 — Input Sanitisation & Error Handling: injection prevention, clean error messages
description: As a security champion, I want all inputs sanitised and all errors to return clean, non-leaking messages so that the service is resistant to injection and information disclosure.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Prompt strings sanitised: null bytes removed, length enforced, encoding validated
- [ ] File paths validated against workspace root (no path traversal)
- [ ] All provider errors mapped to MCP error objects (no stack traces exposed)
- [ ] Unknown tool names return standard MCP method-not-found error
- [ ] Unit test: path traversal attempt → rejected with clear error
- [ ] Unit test: null byte in prompt → sanitised or rejected

title: US-015 — MCP Roots: workspace-aware file saving for generated images
description: As an MCP server, I want to discover the client's workspace roots so that generated images can be saved to the user's project directory when save_to_workspace=true.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] roots/list sent during tool call when save_to_workspace: true
- [ ] Only triggered when client declares roots capability
- [ ] Images saved to first matching root with timestamped filename
- [ ] Saved file path returned in tool result
- [ ] Unit test: roots/list response mapped to workspace path correctly
- [ ] Unit test: no roots capability → save_to_workspace gracefully returns base64 only
- [ ] Integration test: save_to_workspace=true → file written to mock workspace root
- [ ] Security test: path traversal in root URI rejected

title: US-014 — MCP Sampling: prompt enhancement via client LLM
description: As an MCP server, I want to use the client LLM to enhance image prompts before calling the API so that image quality improves without requiring server-side LLM credentials.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Sampling only triggered when client declares sampling capability
- [ ] Sampling only sent during active tools/call requests
- [ ] Sampling request asks LLM to produce a detailed image generation prompt
- [ ] Enriched prompt replaces/augments original before API call
- [ ] Sampling opt-in via USE_SAMPLING env var (default: true)
- [ ] Unit test: sampling request message format correct per MCP spec
- [ ] Unit test: sampling disabled → original prompt used directly
- [ ] Integration test: sampling response → enriched prompt → API called with it

title: US-010 — image_edit tool: base64/file image + mask, prompt-based editing
description: As an MCP client, I want to call image_edit with an image, optional mask, and prompt so that I receive an edited version of the image.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Tool registered with Zod schema (FR-002 parameters)
- [ ] Accepts base64-encoded image and mask inputs
- [ ] Accepts file path (resolved against workspace root if available)
- [ ] Returns edited image(s) with metadata
- [ ] Unit test: valid call → correct API parameters mapped
- [ ] Unit test: invalid base64 → clear error message
- [ ] Integration test: full tool call → mocked API → edited image returned

title: US-013 — MCP Elicitation: interactive parameter refinement via form mode
description: As an MCP server, I want to request clarifying parameters from the user when a tool call is ambiguous so that image generation results match user intent without the user needing to know all parameters upfront.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Elicitation only triggered when client declares elicitation capability
- [ ] Elicitation only sent during active tools/call requests (never standalone)
- [ ] Form mode requests: image style, aspect ratio, quality level
- [ ] Server NEVER requests passwords, API keys, or secrets via form mode (security test)
- [ ] Unit test: elicitation request schema is valid per MCP spec
- [ ] Unit test: capability not declared → elicitation skipped gracefully
- [ ] Integration test: ambiguous call → elicitation → user response → image generated
- [ ] Security test: form mode schema contains no password/secret/credential fields

title: US-004 — Streamable HTTP Transport: POST /mcp, MCP_API_KEY auth
description: As a backend engineer, I want the MCP server to listen on a Streamable HTTP endpoint so that multiple MCP clients can connect simultaneously.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] POST /mcp accepts JSON-RPC 2.0 MCP requests
- [ ] initialize handshake returns correct server info and capabilities
- [ ] tools/list returns tool registry (populated in M3)
- [ ] PORT env var controls listening port (default: 3000)
- [ ] MCP_API_KEY env var, if set, requires Authorization: Bearer <key>
- [ ] Integration test: initialize → tools/list returns 200 with valid MCP response
- [ ] Integration test: missing/wrong API key returns 401

title: US-008 — Azure OpenAI Provider Adapter: AzureOpenAI client, deployment routing
description: As a backend engineer, I want an Azure OpenAI provider adapter so that image generation calls can be routed to Azure AI Foundry deployments.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] AzureOpenAIProvider implements IImageProvider interface
- [ ] Uses AZURE_OPENAI_ENDPOINT, AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY, AZURE_OPENAI_DEPLOYMENT, AZURE_OPENAI_API_VERSION
- [ ] Uses AzureOpenAI client from openai npm package
- [ ] Deployment name used as model identifier in API calls
- [ ] API version defaults to 2025-04-01-preview
- [ ] Unit tests mirror US-007 tests with Azure-specific config
- [ ] Unit test: missing AZURE_OPENAI_ENDPOINT → validation error at startup

title: US-009 — image_generate tool: full Zod schema, provider integration, streaming
description: As an MCP client, I want to call image_generate with a prompt so that I receive a base64-encoded image and metadata.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Tool registered with Zod schema for all FR-001 parameters
- [ ] Correct annotations (readOnlyHint:false, openWorldHint:true, etc.)
- [ ] Returns base64 image(s) in markdown format by default
- [ ] Returns structured JSON when response_format=json
- [ ] Streaming mode returns partial images as progress
- [ ] Unit test: valid prompt → correct API call parameters
- [ ] Unit test: n > 10 → Zod validation error
- [ ] Unit test: prompt > 32000 chars → Zod validation error
- [ ] Unit test: dall-e-3 with n > 1 → API-level error surfaced clearly
- [ ] Integration test: full tool call → mocked OpenAI → base64 image returned

title: US-007 — OpenAI Provider Adapter: generate, edit, validate; secret masking
description: As a backend engineer, I want an OpenAI provider adapter wrapping the openai npm SDK so that image generation calls are routed to OpenAI's API with full error handling.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] OpenAIProvider implements IImageProvider interface
- [ ] Uses OPENAI_API_KEY and optional OPENAI_BASE_URL from ConfigService
- [ ] generate() calls client.images.generate() with mapped parameters
- [ ] edit() calls client.images.edit() with mapped parameters
- [ ] validate() performs lightweight API connectivity check
- [ ] API key is NEVER logged (unit test asserts this)
- [ ] Unit test (mocked HTTP): valid call → ImagesResponse returned
- [ ] Unit test (mocked HTTP): 429 → RateLimitError with retry guidance
- [ ] Unit test (mocked HTTP): 401 → AuthenticationError with clear message

title: US-006 — MCP Capabilities Declaration: tools, elicitation, logging
description: As a software architect, I want the server to correctly declare its MCP capabilities so that clients know what protocol features are available.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] initialize response includes tools: {} capability
- [ ] initialize response includes elicitation: {} when elicitation is enabled
- [ ] Capabilities configurable via env vars (can disable elicitation/sampling)
- [ ] Unit test: capability object matches expected structure per MCP spec

title: M4 — MCP Advanced: Elicitation, Sampling, Roots
description: Advanced MCP protocol features: Elicitation for interactive parameter refinement, Sampling for prompt enhancement via client LLM, Roots for workspace-aware file saving.

title: US-002 — CI Pipeline: GitHub Actions (lint → type-check → test → build)
description: As a DevOps engineer, I want a GitHub Actions CI pipeline on every push and PR so that quality gates are enforced automatically.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Pipeline runs: lint → type-check → unit tests → build
- [ ] Pipeline fails PR if any step fails
- [ ] Coverage report uploaded as artifact
- [ ] Runs on ubuntu-latest with Bun installed
- [ ] Secrets injected via GitHub Secrets (never hardcoded)

title: M5 — Production Ready: Security, observability, rate limiting
description: Rate limiting, structured JSON logs, Prometheus metrics, health endpoints, input sanitisation, and error handling polish.

title: M6 — Container & Delivery: Docker, CI/CD, Helm
description: Multi-stage Dockerfile, GitHub Actions pipeline (lint → test → build → scan → publish), OCI image to ghcr.io, optional Helm chart.

title: US-001 — Project scaffold: Bun + NestJS + TypeScript strict
description: As a backend engineer, I want a working Bun + NestJS project skeleton with TypeScript strict mode so that I can start implementing modules with a consistent structure.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] bun install succeeds
- [ ] bun run build produces dist/ directory
- [ ] bun run test runs with zero failures
- [ ] bun run lint passes with zero warnings
- [ ] bun run start starts the app and logs "Application started"
- [ ] .env.example documents all required environment variables
- [ ] src/ follows the module structure defined in ARCHITECTURE.md
TDD: Write smoke test app.spec.ts asserting app bootstraps — before writing AppModule.

title: US-003 — Environment Config Module: validated env vars, fail-fast startup
description: As a backend engineer, I want a validated configuration module that reads env vars at startup so that the app fails fast with a clear error if required config is missing.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] ConfigModule validates all required env vars at startup
- [ ] Missing PROVIDER → refuses to start: "PROVIDER is required (openai|azure)"
- [ ] Missing provider credentials → refuses to start with provider-specific message
- [ ] All config values accessible via injected ConfigService
- [ ] Unit test: missing PROVIDER triggers validation error
- [ ] Unit test: all required fields present → no error

title: M2 — MCP Core: Server + transports + provider adapters
description: A running MCP server clients can connect to, with both Streamable HTTP and stdio transports, correct capability declaration, and both provider adapters (OpenAI + Azure).

title: M3 — Image Tools: image_generate, image_edit, image_variation, provider tools
description: All five MCP tools fully implemented with Zod schemas, annotations, provider integration, unit tests, and integration tests.

title: M1 — Foundation: Project scaffold, CI, config validation
description: Lay the groundwork. Everything that must exist before any feature code is written: Bun+NestJS skeleton, CI pipeline, and validated env configuration module.

title: US-011 — image_variation tool: dall-e-2 only, variation generation
description: As an MCP client, I want to call image_variation with an existing image so that I receive a creative variation of that image.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Tool registered with Zod schema (FR-003 parameters)
- [ ] Only valid for dall-e-2 (other models return clear error)
- [ ] Returns variation image(s) with metadata
- [ ] Unit test: non-dall-e-2 model → error "image_variation only supports dall-e-2"
- [ ] Integration test: full tool call → mocked API → variation returned

title: US-012 — provider_list and provider_validate tools
description: As an MCP client, I want to query provider status so that I know which providers are available before generating images.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] provider_list returns all configured providers with status (available/degraded/error)
- [ ] provider_list annotations: readOnlyHint:true, idempotentHint:true
- [ ] provider_validate tests connectivity and returns pass/fail with details
- [ ] provider_validate annotations: readOnlyHint:true, openWorldHint:true
- [ ] Unit tests for both tools covering success and failure paths

title: US-005 — stdio Transport: MCP_TRANSPORT=stdio, no stdout logging
description: As a backend engineer, I want the MCP server to support stdio transport so that it can be used as a subprocess by local MCP clients (Claude Desktop, Goose).
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] MCP_TRANSPORT=stdio switches to stdio mode
- [ ] Server does NOT log to stdout in stdio mode (stderr only)
- [ ] initialize handshake works over stdio
- [ ] Unit test: transport factory creates correct transport based on MCP_TRANSPORT
